name: Barbell Back Squat
cues: Brace your core before descending
Keep chest tall, don't let torso collapse forward
Drive knees out in line with toes
Descend until hips are at or below parallel
Drive through heels on the way up

name: Conventional Deadlift
cues: Bar over mid-foot, hip-width stance
Hinge at hips first, then bend knees to grip
Lat engagement: imagine bending the bar around your legs
Push the floor away, don't think about pulling
Lock hips and knees simultaneously at the top

name: Bench Press
cues: Arch upper back, shoulder blades retracted and depressed
Feet flat on floor, full-body tension
Grip slightly wider than shoulder-width
Lower bar to lower chest, elbows at 45-60 degrees
Drive bar up and slightly back over face

name: Pull-Up
cues: Dead hang to start with full arm extension
Depress shoulder blades before pulling
Drive elbows toward hips, not back
Chin clears the bar at the top
Control the descent over 2-3 seconds

name: Overhead Press
muscle_group: Anterior Deltoids, Triceps, Upper Traps, Core
cues: Grip just outside shoulder width
Squeeze glutes and brace core throughout
Push head through the window as bar passes face
Bar path should be slightly arc, not straight vertical
Lock out elbows fully at the top

name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
cues: Soft knee bend, hinge from hip not waist
Keep dumbbells close to legs throughout
Feel stretch in hamstrings before reversing
Neutral spine, don't round lower back
Drive hips forward to return to standing

name: Cable Row
muscle_group: Rhomboids, Mid Traps, Latissimus Dorsi, Biceps
cues: Sit tall with slight forward lean at start
Lead with elbows, not hands
Squeeze shoulder blades together at full contraction
Avoid using momentum by swinging torso
Full stretch at the front of each rep

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
cues: Rear foot elevated on bench, laces down
Front foot far enough forward so knee doesn't go past toes excessively
Maintain upright torso throughout
Lower hips straight down, not forward
Push through front heel to stand

name: Plank
cues: Forearms on floor, elbows under shoulders
Body forms a straight line from head to heels
Squeeze glutes and quads to prevent hip sag
Breathe steadily, don't hold breath
Avoid letting hips rise above shoulder level

name: Hip Thrust
cues: Upper back rested on bench edge at shoulder blade level
Feet flat, hip-width apart
Drive through heels, squeeze glutes at top
Chin tucked slightly, no hyperextension of spine
Hold contraction 1-2 seconds at peak

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
cues: Slight forward lean from hips
Lead with elbows, pinkies slightly up
Raise to shoulder height only, not higher
Control the descent — don't let weights drop
Avoid shrugging shoulders during the lift

name: Tricep Dip
cues: Keep torso upright for tricep focus (leaning forward hits chest more)
Lower until upper arms are parallel to floor
Don't flare elbows excessively outward
Press through palms fully at the top
Add weight via belt for progressive overload

name: Barbell Curl
cues: Stand tall, elbows pinned to sides throughout
Supinate wrists fully at the top
Don't swing torso — isolate the bicep
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase
Full extension at the bottom each rep

name: Face Pull
cues: Set cable at head height or slightly above
Pull rope toward face, hands beside ears at the end
Elbows should be at or above shoulder level
Focus on external rotation of shoulders
Great for shoulder health and posture correction

name: Goblet Squat
cues: Hold weight at chest height close to body
Feet slightly wider than hips, toes turned out 30 degrees
Sit between heels, deep squat depth
Elbows inside knees at the bottom to push them out
Great beginner squat patterning exercise

name: Lat Pulldown
cues: Grip slightly wider than shoulder width
Lean back 10-15 degrees, chest up
Pull bar to upper chest, drive elbows down and back
Squeeze lats hard at full contraction
Slow return to full arm extension

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
cues: Bench set at 30-45 degrees
Dumbbells start at shoulder level, palms forward
Press up and slightly together at the top
Don't touch dumbbells at the top — maintain tension
Lower slowly, feel the upper chest stretch

name: Running (Steady State)
cues: Land with foot under center of mass, not out in front
Slight forward lean from ankles, not waist
Relax shoulders and jaw — don't tense up
Arms swing forward-backward, not across body
Aim for 170-180 steps per minute cadence

name: Kettlebell Swing
cues: Hip hinge pattern, not a squat
Hike bell back between legs before each rep
Explosive hip snap is the engine — arms just guide
Bell floats to shoulder height, not overhead
Squeeze glutes hard at the top position

name: Mountain Climber
cues: Start in push-up position, wrists under shoulders
Keep hips level — don't let them rise
Drive knees toward chest alternately in rapid succession
Maintain a rigid plank throughout the torso
Increase speed for a cardio effect

name: Jump Rope
cues: Land softly on balls of feet, not flat-footed
Small efficient jumps — just enough to clear the rope
Keep elbows close to body, wrists do the work
Look straight ahead, not at your feet
Start with 30-second intervals if new to jumping

name: Box Jump
cues: Stand arm's length from box
Quarter squat, swing arms back, then explode up
Land softly with both feet simultaneously
Hips and knees bent on landing — absorb impact
Step down (don't jump down) to protect joints

name: Farmers Walk
cues: Stand tall, shoulders pulled back and down
Take short, quick controlled steps
Brace core as if bracing for a punch
Don't let the weight pull you to one side
Excellent full-body finisher or functional strength movement

name: Push-Up
cues: Hands slightly wider than shoulder-width
Body forms a straight line from head to heels
Lower until chest nearly touches floor
Elbows at 45-degree angle to torso, not flared out
Full lockout at the top without losing core tension

name: Nordic Curl
cues: Kneel with feet anchored, body upright
Lower body toward floor as slowly as possible
Keep hips extended — don't bend at the hip
Use hands to catch yourself at the bottom
Use minimal push from hands to return to start
